Paper Title/Study: Impact of Cowpea-Based Food Containing Fish Meal Served With Vitamin C–Rich Drink on Iron Stores and Hemoglobin Concentrations in Ghanaian Schoolchildren in a Malaria Endemic Area
Authors: Godfred Egbi, Irene Ayi, Firibu Kwesi Saalia, Francis Zotor, Theodosia Adom, Eric Harrison, Collins K. Ahorlu, Matilda Steiner-Asiedu
Diet Type: Cowpea-based diet with/without fish meal and vitamin C supplementation
Food Component: cowpea-based food, fish meal, vitamin C-rich drink
Biomarkers: hemoglobin concentration, serum ferritin, complement-reactive protein (CRP), malaria parasitemia, soil-transmitted helminthes
Outcome / Finding: Improved hemoglobin concentration and reduced prevalence of anemia.
Method: Randomized controlled trial (3 groups: fish meal-vitamin C, vitamin C, control)
Contribution: Problem: To evaluate the effectiveness of cowpea-based food containing fish meal served with vitamin C–rich drink to improve iron stores and hemoglobin concentrations in Ghanaian schoolchildren.
Contribution: Result: Cowpea-based food containing 3% fish meal and served with vitamin C–rich drink improved hemoglobin concentration and minimized the prevalence of anemia.

Paper Title/Study: Iron deficiency due to consumption of a habitual diet low in bioavailable iron: a longitudinal cohort study in Moroccan children
